source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of America - Viosca Knoll Block 989

actions_taken: ROV deployed to investigate suspected gas release
Engineering evaluation initiated for repair options
Repair plan to be submitted to BSEE for approval
event.summary: On 02 December 2024 at approximately 0830 hours, a gas release was detected during pressurization testing of a newly replaced Subsea Control Module (SCM) pod at VK 989 Platform A. A Remote Operated Vehicle (ROV) identified gas bubbles emanating from a 90-degree bend segment in subsea flowline pipeline segment 10688. The flowline was immediately isolated and pressure testing suspended. The incident is suspected to have resulted from corrosion at a previous field repair location where field welds may have had imperfections.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District has no recommendations for Office of Incident Investigations at this time

hazards.name: Pressurized hydrocarbon gas in subsea pipeline

threats.name: Pipeline structural failure due to corrosion at field repair location
Inadequate field repair workmanship and quality control
Loss of pipeline integrity during pressure testing

consequences.name: Pipeline asset damage and loss of production

controls.name: Pressure testing of flowline during SCM pod commissioning
controls.lod_basis: Operational testing to verify system integrity before full commissioning

supporting_text: During testing of the control pod, the control room operators received data that indicated there was a possible gas release coming from the flowline
the control room operators received data that indicated there was a possible gas release

controls.name: Remote Operated Vehicle (ROV) investigation and visual inspection
controls.barrier_role: Detection and localization of gas release source
controls.lod_basis: Subsea visual inspection to confirm and locate suspected leak

supporting_text: The ROV was deployed while gas from VK-989 A was used to pressurize the flowline. While the flowline was pressured, the ROV was able to identify gas bubbles coming from the flowline at a 90-degree bend segment in the flowline
the ROV was able to identify gas bubbles coming from the flowline at a 90-degree bend segment

controls.name: Immediate suspension of pressure testing operations
controls.barrier_role: Stop pressurization to prevent further gas release
controls.lod_basis: Operational response to confirmed gas release detection

supporting_text: Once the source of the release was identified by the ROV, VK-989 A suspended all pressure testing and the ROV returned to surface

controls.name: Pipeline segment isolation
controls.barrier_role: Prevent further gas release from affected pipeline segment
controls.lod_basis: Isolation of damaged section to contain gas release

supporting_text: when the gas leak was discovered on 02 December 2024, VK-989 A operators were able to isolate the pipeline segment to mitigate the possibility of further gas leaks occurring from the subsea pipeline segment

controls.name: Field repair quality control and weld inspection procedures
controls.barrier_role: Prevent defective field repairs that lead to corrosion and pipeline failure
controls.lod_basis: Quality assurance during field repair operations

supporting_text: Talos believes that there might have been a repair in the flowline at some point in the past and the field weld that would have been made to repair the flowline could have possibly had imperfections leading to corrosion in the repaired area
Equipment failure: Flawed equipment design or construction- Suspected flaw in the field weld and testing of the weld during suspected past field flowline repair

controls.name: Controlled environment for pipeline repairs
controls.barrier_role: Prevent environmental degradation of field repairs
controls.lod_basis: Work environment control to ensure repair quality

supporting_text: Work environment: Poor layout or design of work area- Suspected repair being performed in an uncontrolled environment allowing for environmental condition to affect the repair that may have been performed
the repair being made in the field instead of in a controlled environment which may have caused the flowline to develop a leak

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
